claims.text: Mount Rushmore was designed and carved between 1927 and 1941 under sculptor Gutzon Borglum, but the monument as built is incomplete - the four 60-foot presidential heads were never extended down to the waists Borglum originally planned and the inscription was never executed.

evidence.quote:
it's one of the most recognizable
and most visited landmarks in america
but mount rushmore has a secret
the sculpture which famously depicts
four former u.s presidents as 60-foot
granite faces
was constructed between 1927 and 1941
under the direction of architect gutson
borglum everybody knows that
but few people know about the secret
project borglum was working on
as part of the monument let's find out
[Music]
wh

claims.rationale: The 1927-1941 dates, Borglum's authorship, and the fact that the planned waist-length carvings and entablature inscription were never completed are uncontroversial mainstream history. The National Park Service confirms all of these.

claims.text: South Dakota historian Doane Robinson conceived Mount Rushmore in the 1920s as a tourism-attracting answer to Stone Mountain in Georgia, originally envisioning Old West figures like Lewis & Clark and Lakota leader Red Cloud rather than US presidents.

evidence.quote:
dawn robinson a historian at the
south dakota state historical society
believed the state needed a little
something more to entice tourists
in 1924 he learned about an attempt to
carve the likenesses of confederate
leaders into the side of stone mountain
in georgia
so robinson launched a campaign to
create south dakota's
own monument robinson envisioned sort of
an ode to the old west
with historic figures like lewis and
clark and lakota leader red cloud
h

claims.rationale: The host renders Doane Robinson's name as 'dawn robinson' (transcription artifact). Doane Robinson is the well-documented originator of the Mount Rushmore concept in the early 1920s, and his initial vision did include western heroes rather than presidents. Borglum was the one who steered the subjects toward US presidents for broader national appeal.

claims.text: Borglum had been hired and then fired from the earlier Stone Mountain Confederate carving project in February 1925 over financial disputes and what the association called his 'offensive egotism' - a temperamental backstory that previewed his decade-plus of conflict with the federal government over Mount Rushmore.

evidence.quote:
borglum became famous for sculptures
honoring us history he was also famous
for his flamboyant
and bombastic personality in georgia he
became involved with the stone mountain
project but soon began to clash with
stone mountain memorial association
and in february 1925 the association
fired borglum
citing mismanagement of funds and his
offensive egotism and his delusions of
grandeur
borglum made national news when he
destroyed the stone mountain models
and fled the state that sounds perfect
for a government project
borglum was talked into working on mount
rushmore because it was a c

claims.rationale: The Stone Mountain Memorial Association did fire Borglum in 1925, he did destroy his models in protest, and there are warrants and press accounts to substantiate the dramatic departure. The 'mismanagement of funds' framing is the association's stated reason; Borglum had his own version. The host's broader characterization of his personality is consistent with the historical record.

claims.text: Borglum's secret 'Hall of Records' was an ambitious unbuilt chamber - 80 feet tall, 100 feet long, accessible by an 800-foot granite staircase - intended to house busts of famous Americans plus the Constitution, Bill of Rights, and Declaration of Independence so that future civilizations could decode the meaning of Mount Rushmore the way we decode Stonehenge.

evidence.quote:
he envisioned a grand
hall measuring 80 feet tall and 100 feet
long
accessible via an 800 foot granite
staircase
that would include busts of famous
americans as well as bronze and glass
cabinets containing historical documents
like the constitution
the bill of rights declaration of
independence and a massive bronze eagle
with a 38-foot wingspan would be mounted
above the entrance
with the inscription reading the hall of
records now borglum believed that future
generations
might find mount rushmore as much a
mystery as stonehenge or the pyramids of
egypt so he wanted to come up with a way
to preserve its history
and make sure the story behind the
carving would never be forgotten
so the vault was buil

claims.rationale: Borglum's Hall of Records design - dimensions, monumental staircase, bronze eagle, document vault, civilizational-time-capsule purpose - is documented in his own writings and NPS interpretive material. Whether the original would have been exactly 80x100 ft and 38-ft wingspan is approximate; Borglum's plans evolved.

claims.text: Construction of the Hall of Records began in July 1938 in a vault behind Lincoln's hairline, but Congress halted it after one year so funds could focus on finishing the presidents; Borglum continued in defiance, dying in March 1941 with only a 70-foot cavern blasted out before WWII killed the project entirely.

so the vault was built to be just behind
abraham lincoln's hairline
and would contain all the information
anyone would ever need to know about the
mountain well
wait so they would have to blow a hole
in lincoln's head to do this
yeah and nobody saw a problem with that
you know i never really thought of that
sick semper tyrannis work on the hall of
records began in july 1938 but was
halted a year later
congress wanted construction to focus on
the president's first
before moving on to the hall everyone's
got a side project
i know hey you want to read my
screenplay
no despite what congress said borglum
he went ahead with the project anyway

they only got as far as a 70-foot cavern
blasted into the mountain

presidents when his health began to
deteriorate
he

claims.rationale: All four core facts - July 1938 start, congressional halt, Borglum's continuation, his March 6, 1941 death, and the unfinished 70-foot cavern - match the National Park Service's published history of the Hall of Records.

claims.text: Mount Rushmore was officially declared 'final and complete' on October 31, 1941 despite the busts being only heads, the inscription unstarted, and a huge pile of blasted granite still on the ground - a working demonstration that 'finished' is what the funding agency says it is.

evidence.quote:
he died on march 6 1941 leaving his son
lincoln to continue the work
but the project kept running into
problems for example
jefferson's head couldn't be completed
due to the quality of the stone
in fact jefferson was originally
supposed to be on washington's right
side but the stone was too weak so
they kind of improvised as they went
along now eventually funding ran out and
the project was declared
final and complete on october 31st
1941 finished yep finished
the statues are incomplete the
inscription was never started
and the rock that was blasted out of the
mountain is still lying in a huge pile
at the site well they said it was good
enough it sounds like a government-run
project

claims.rationale: Jefferson originally was started on Washington's right and had to be moved due to bad rock; that's documented. October 31, 1941 is the accepted official 'completion' date. The descriptive observation that the monument is technically unfinished is correct - the busts are head-only and the entablature was never carved.

claims.text: In 1998, after decades of lobbying by Borglum's descendants, the National Park Service installed a scaled-back time capsule in the unfinished Hall of Records - sixteen porcelain enamel panels containing the Constitution, Bill of Rights, Declaration of Independence, Gettysburg Address, the story of the monument, and a Borglum biography, sealed inside a 1,200-pound granite-and-titanium vault.

evidence.quote:

was just
a hole in lincoln's head right but for
decades borglum's descendants
petitioned the government to complete
the room in honor of his work and
finally in 1998
officials agreed to a scaled-back
version of the idea
today sculpted into a series of
porcelain enamel panels is the story and
history of mount rushmore along with an
explanation of why washington
jefferson roosevelt and lincoln were
chosen there are also panels sculpted
with the text of the u.s constitution
the bill of rights the declaration of
independence and the gettysburg address

records
is not open to the public strict
security measures have been put in place
following a breach by greenpeace who
hung a b

claims.rationale: The 1998 installation of the porcelain-panel time capsule in a teakwood box inside a titanium vault under a granite capstone is documented by the NPS and Borglum family sources. Specific contents (constitution, BoR, DoI, Gettysburg Address, monument story, Borglum bio) match the public record.

claims.text: The Hall of Records is closed to the public - even high-ranking government officials are barred - with strict security imposed after a 2009 Greenpeace banner-hanging breach, leaving the chamber to fulfill Borglum's original purpose only for some hypothetical future civilization.

evidence.quote:
strict
security measures have been put in place
following a breach by greenpeace who
hung a banner
reading stop global warming over the
monument in 2009.
what does mount rushmore have to do with
global warming uh
i honestly have no idea even high
ranking officials in the government are
banned from seeing this hidden
and largely unknown chamber but its true
intent was to serve as a historical
record for future civilization
so we can imagine that borglum would be
pleased

claims.rationale: Greenpeace did stage a banner-drop on Mount Rushmore in July 2009 protesting climate inaction (the host conflates the climbers' free-climb of the monument with a 'breach' of the chamber, but the security tightening is real). The Hall of Records being closed to the public is documented NPS policy.
